sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.8 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: b) Let$G$be any Lie group (indeed, any separable continuous group) made discrete: can$G$act faithfully on a countable set? Comment of 2021: an affirmative answer was obtained for the nilpotent case (N.\,Monod, J.~Group Theory, { 25}, no.\,5 (2022), 851--865). Comment of 2025: this problem is reduced to the case of simple Lie groups; in particular, the solvable case is settled (A.\,Conversano, N.\,...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
